title: Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup

seo.title: Authentic Tuscan Ribollita Recipe (Twice-Boiled Soup)
seo.metaDescription: Discover how to make authentic Tuscan Ribollita, the classic Italian twice-boiled soup. This hearty, traditional recipe is packed with vegetables, beans, and bread.
longTailKeywords: how to make Tuscan Ribollita soup
seo.h1: Authentic Tuscan Ribollita Recipe: Classic Italian Twice-Boiled Soup
h2Headings: How to Make Tuscan Ribollita: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Ribollita Soup
seo.imageAlt: A rustic bowl of authentic Tuscan Ribollita soup, garnished with fresh parsley and a drizzle of olive oil.
seo.imageCaption: Savor the rich flavors of traditional Tuscan Ribollita, a hearty and comforting Italian peasant soup.

description: Ribollita is a quintessential Tuscan peasant soup, a hearty and robust dish that perfectly embodies the region's cucina povera philosophy. This 'twice-boiled' soup is packed with an abundance of seasonal vegetables, cannellini beans, and stale bread, creating a wonderfully thick and comforting meal. It is a true taste of Tuscan tradition, perfect for a chilly evening.

ingredients.notes: rinsed and drained, reserving 1/2 cup for mashing

instructions.title: Prepare the Soffritto
instructions.text: In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the finely chopped onion, carrots, and celery. Cook, stirring occasionally, until softened and translucent, about 8-10 minutes. This forms the flavor base, known as soffritto.

instructions.title: Add Garlic, Tomatoes, and Beans
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Add the diced tomatoes with their juices and bring to a gentle simmer. Mash about half a cup of the rinsed cannellini beans with a fork or potato masher, then add all the beans (mashed and whole) to the pot. This helps thicken the soup.

instructions.title: Incorporate Greens and Broth
instructions.text: Pour in the vegetable broth. Add the chopped black kale and shredded green cabbage. Bring the soup to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for at least 20-25 minutes, or until the vegetables are tender. Season with salt and freshly ground black pepper to your liking.

instructions.title: First Boil and Bread Addition
instructions.text: Once the vegetables are tender, remove the lid. Add the torn stale Tuscan bread pieces directly into the soup, pushing them down gently to submerge. Continue to simmer for another 10-15 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the bread to absorb the liquid and thicken the soup. The soup should become very thick and stew-like.

instructions.title: The 'Second Boil' and Resting
instructions.text: Turn off the heat. This is where the 'twice-boiled' aspect comes in: traditionally, Ribollita is left to rest and cool completely, sometimes even overnight, before being reheated. This allows the flavors to meld and the bread to fully integrate, making the soup even thicker and more flavorful. If serving immediately, let it rest for at least 15-20 minutes off the heat.

instructions.title: Serve and Garnish
instructions.text: When ready to serve, gently reheat the soup over low heat if it has cooled. Ladle the hearty Ribollita into bowls. Drizzle generously with fresh extra virgin olive oil and sprinkle with chopped fresh parsley. A little extra black pepper is also a nice touch. Serve hot as a satisfying main course.

tips: For the most authentic flavor, use a hearty, crusty, day-old Tuscan bread. Avoid soft sandwich bread, as it will dissolve too quickly.
Ribollita is even better the next day! The flavors deepen and the soup thickens further upon resting, truly earning its 'twice-boiled' name. Make a large batch.
Feel free to customize the vegetables based on seasonality. Swiss chard, spinach, or even potatoes can be added, but kale and cabbage are traditional.
article: Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup is a beloved Italian Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
